IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: To enhance the self-esteem of children in need by providing them targeted financial assistance.
IRS990.MissionDesc: To enhance the self-esteem of children in need by providing them targeted financial assistance.
IRS990.Desc: Alices Kids anonymously pays for items such as a field trip, prom ticket, new shirt, soccer cleats, eyeglasses, via a check or gift card to the requestor. In 2025, Alices Kids served 14,232 children by responding to requsts from a national network of teachers and social workers across the country. Alices Kids strives to lift childrens self esteem by paying for items or services that others may take for granted.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: Other Income Part II, Line 10 Description: Credit card rewards 2023: 7315. 2024: 10407. Description: Restitution 2025: 42000. Description: Other income 2025: 1660.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Part I, Line 1 -
Founded in 2011, Alices Kids exists to confront a hidden
reality of childhood poverty; lack of resources and the
quiet, public moments that shape a childs sense of
belonging and self-worth.
For many children, poverty shows up in deeply visible
ways: wearing the same worn clothes day after day,
missing out on field trips, skipping school to avoid
stares/comments. These moments can lead to
embarrassment, bullying, and isolation. At Alices Kids,
we believe the most painful part of poverty is the loss
of dignity.
Our mission is simple: remove the financial barriers
behind those moments - quickly, discreetly, and without
stigma.
Alices Kids fund the little things that make a big
difference in a childs life. Field trip fees, shoes,
eyeglasses, school supplies, prom tickets,
extracurricular activities, and more. While modest in
cost, these items often determine whether a child feels
included or left out.
What sets Alices Kids apart is how the help is
delivered. We do not require families to complete
applications, submit financial documents, or share
personal hardships. Instead, requests come from a
trusted nationwide network of teachers, social workers,
law enforcement personnel, CASA advocates, and other
community professionals who understand the childs
needs firsthand. Most requests are fulfilled within 24-
48 hours.
Support is provided in a way that perserves the dignity
of both the child and their family. Parents or
caregivers are given a gift card, and then take the child
shopping or pay for a trip without the child ever
knowing they received charity. In these moments, the
parent becomes the hero, and the child simply
experiences the joy of being a kid.
What began as a small grassroots effort has grown into
a nationwide movement of quiet impact. In 2012, Alices
Kids raised approximately $14,000. In 2025, the
Organization surpassed $1 million in annual donations and
supported more than 14,000 children across all 50 states.
2025 was our most successful year to date.
* Alices Kids saw a 37% increase of requests over 2024.
* We received the highest possible rating from Charity
Navigator.
* We paid $175,000 for more than 800 high school seniors
who lost their homes to the Palisades fires in California
to go to the prom. Actor Steve Carell helped with a
video announcement during senior assemblies where the
kids were stunned!
* We garnered more than 75 unique articles in media
throughout the country, including mentions in the Today
Show, ABC Nightly News with David Muir, CBS Mornings, The
Washington Post and the Los Angeles Times.
* Fairfax County Public Schools (Virginia) awarded
Alices Kids a proclaimation commending our work for kids
across the country.
* We had a lot of fun with movie star Kevin Bacon on his
podcast. Executive Director Ron Fitzsimmons, along with
comedian and supporter Patton Oswalt, talked to Kevin
about our work.
* Speaking of celebrities, we recently got the support of
Stephanie Courtney, otherwise known as Flo in the
Progressive Insurance ads.
Behind every number is a student able to attend a once
out-of-reach academic program, art supplies that blossom
into a growing talent, a teen gaining confidence by
interviewing in a new suit, and educators witnessing
renewed confidence in students who no longer fell left
out.
Here are some testimonials from requestors on how Alices
Kids has helped their students:
* I want to tell you that the first support I received
from you was a soccer uniform for a student on the wrong
track, but desperately wanting to play soccer. He now
dedicates himself to sports and his studies. He has made
a 180-degree change - it brings a lot of joy to see
everything he has achieved!
* I am very grateful for the work and support that your
organization gives us. In my role as family liason, I
have learned that our children not only need food and
clothing, but to show them that we believe in them - it
is often the impetus they need to break all limits and
fulfill their dreams. Without your help and support,
stories like these would not be possible.
* Thank you all so much for these incredible services,
truly life-saving! While I dont have any photos of my
kiddos using their items, I can confidently say that
youve made a lasting impact on their lives. One child
received a fishing pole, allowing him and his stepdad to
rebuild their bond. Others received bikes and musical
instruments, bringing joy and new opportunities into
their lives. Your support has done more than just
provide items; it has helped reconnect families and kids
with each other and with their communities. We are
beyond grateful. Thank you all for your hard work and
dedication!
* I would love to thank you all for your support of our
families this year! I was able to give (I believe) five
gift cards, and the families were beyond appreciative!
I love your goal of helping kids feel like they fit in by
not being outed for their inability to pay for things.
The fifth grader who proudly wore a new outfit for
promotion; the fifth grader who was able to have a party;
and now the fourth grader who gets to have his own room
with indiviualized decor! I cannot thank you enough! I
love what you do and am able to see the appreciation in
cargivers eyes!
* Just wanted to send a quick note of thanks. The
experience was something I dont think they will ever
forget. Thankful for this organization that helps combat
the helpless feeling that sometimes creeps up in working
families living in extreme poverty in rural areas. You
have been instrumental in filling the chasm of support in
remote areas. Thank you!
Alices Kids continues to expand it reach while staying
true to its founding belief: when dignity is protected,
everything else becomes possible. Because we know that a
little help goes a long way.
The board president, secretary, executive director and
another director have a family relationship.
The 990 draft is issued to each board member for review
prior to filing the return.
Board members are asked to review the by-laws including
including the conflict of interest policy annually.
Board members make disclosures and sign a form disclosing
any relationships.
Documents are available upon request.
The board reviews the executive directors compensation
annually. They compare compensation to similar sized
non-profits in the region reported on the 990s via
Guidestar.
